titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Board of Trustees records2-3-74
titlestmt.author: Compiled by C. Rote, B. Huth et al.

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-09-22 23:59:22 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Board of Trustees records
did.abstract: Minutes and work folders for Board of Trustees and Executive Committee meetings.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Compiled by:
defitem.item: C. Rote, B. Huth et al.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Minutes and work folders for Board of Trustees and Executive Committee meetings.

emph.ref: Series IV. Council on the College of Veterinary Medicine
Series VI. Agricultural College Council and State College Council
Series VII. Committee on Buildings and Grounds
Series VIII. Finance and Budget Committees

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: For the published Proceedings of the Board of Trustees of Cornell University see Archives LD1341.A1.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Board of Trustees records, #2-3-74. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted for 50 years from date of creation or by permission of the office of origin. Boxes 80-95 restricted for 75 years from date of creation or permission of the University Archivist. Additional folders marked "access restrictions apply" restricted for 75 years from date of creation or permission of the University Archivist. Refer to finding aid for list of individually restricted folders. For other material less than 75 years old consult with University Archives staff.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Reproductions may be made from the unrestricted material, but may not be published without the permission of the Secretary of the Corporation or the Associate Secretary of the Corporation.

controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Executive Committee -- : Board of Trustees.

p.emph: Files include resolutions, budget information and treasurers reports, correspondence, graduate candidate recommendations, faculty appointments, land reports, and ballots

did.unittitle: Ledger book labeled Cornell University Reports Volume 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand written copies of proceedings of trustee meetings of the New York State Agricultural College and letters of significance to the founding of the University. Proceedings of meetings, on September 20, 1864, January 12, 1865, letters and reports to the trustees of Cornell University 1866-1868 regarding the committee on buildings, the New York State legislature, land script, building funds, copy of resolution regarding Genesee College and payment from Ezra Cornell. Report of the committee on organization submitted for the committee by Andrew D. White, discussing the subjects to be taught and needed professors of various subjects, how to find and recruit professors to Cornell, salaries, discussion of campus collections, observatory, library, military education, religion, etc.. Report regarding proposed theological seminary at Cornell. Letter from James Peck regarding music education, July 29, 1868. Letter from Charles Folger regarding land script, July 30, 1868. Inside cover has a letter to librarian George Harris from a Mr. Wilson January 26, 1897, saying the volume is from E.C. Russell, the son of Cornell Vice President William C. Russell, giving him the volume. Harris notes he presented the volume to President Schurman February 8, 1897.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes university budget breakdown, Ezra Cornell donation resolution, invitation, and trustee election

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on a theological seminary, "Notes for a Plan of Organization of the Military Department"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Anthon Library purchase

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes recommendation letters, memorandum of Military Organization Provisioned, Ezra Cornell sale of land scrips, supplication for the Institution of a Chair of Poetry and Music

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Binocular Telescope letter and instructions, college land scrips fund

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter describing conditions of Cascadilla, signatures of Cascadilla Place stock holders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal for medical department, resignation of board member William Kelly

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes professor resignations and letter on Brazil expedition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter on Brazil Expedition, library report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes White's resolution on the establishment of Sage Women's College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal of reorganization of the College of Chemistry and Physics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes meeting of the Commissioners of the Land Office, land purchases

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolutions about Ezra Cornell's death and burial, Cornell water supply, memorial tablet for John Stanton Gould, railroad, and Woodward/Cornell suit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Woodward/Cornell suit information, resolution that no married people can be admitted to the university without permission from the Executive Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes commencement program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution from the Alumni Association calling for increased salaries of the assistant professors, petition for the return of Professor Byerly, donation of shells for the museum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding Professor Morris's Barn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Stewart Woodford, petition from students on behalf of Professor Morris, description of charges of incompetancy against Professor Morris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from William Russell, resolution from the student body and student petition against the resignation of Professor Russell, submission of grievances from the Alumni Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of lectures on the "Age of Reform in England" at Cornell, copy of Assembly Act No 177, Medical Department plan of Organization, Communications of the NY Association of Cornell to the Board regarding alumni voting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution for a memorial to Ezra Cornell, John McGraw, and Jennie McGraw Fiske; resolution to establish a medical department in New York City, printed copies of resolutions establishing a Museum Council and Library Council

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolutions of the New England Association of Cornell Alumni, resignation of Willard Fiske, and printed resolutions establishing fellowships, scholarships, and a loan fund

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolutions regarding the granting of degrees to students who did not complete their course, B.G. Wilder appointment as curator of Natural History, resolution to appoint a matron for Sage College and to set aside endowment money for Sage Women Scholarships

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from Sibley students and printed circular, and Act to establish a Board of Pharmacy, letter from students about university advertisements with cyanotype photograph of the advertisements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on the erection of a monument to Ezra Cornell, apointment of Agnes Derkheim as Principal of Sage College, New Orleans Exhibition, alumni petition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on the conditions of Sibley, Professor Jones' petition, and a printed copy of A.D. White's resignation, printed petition of Cornell female alumni against compulsory residence at Sage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the plans for a University Library Building, working copy of an indenture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes land report, appropriations recommendations, printed petition from alumni against Honorable Degrees,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from A.D. White offering his library of over 30,000 volumes, sympathies for death of Mrs. A.D. White, report of the Committee to Procure Plans for a Library Building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes heating report, proposed library building studies, proposed Law School report, report on State Scholarships, resolution that stone be used for buildings on the arts quad

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on Rhetoric and English Philology chair, many petitions of students against the raise of tuition, printed proposal for testing machines at Sibley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memorandum regarding the reorganization of the Department of Philosophy, department reports, amendments proposed to the Statutes submitted to the Trustees, organization of the University Senate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution to improve road conditions, printed report on Sibley College, printed plan and alumni resolutions for self-government for Sage College, resignation letter from C.K. Adams and trustee resolutions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution for campus railway, statements on and objections to having street cars on campus, campus railway specifications, petitions to have railway on campus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes equipment need for the Department of Minerology and Petrography, information on the campus railway and danger of electric disturbances, act to establish a state veterinary college at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes official printed report of RH Thurston, scholarship for undergraduate woman submitted by Anna Botsford Comstock and Mary Fowler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes official printed report of RH Thurston, AD White letter requesting a continued lease on the President's House, Mr. Fairweather bequeathment, petition for route of Railway, AD White proposal for the building of Sage stone bridge and gate across Cascadilla

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed information about library circulars and withdrawals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Guiteau student loan fund, death of Alonzo Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information on the Carnegie Foundation, copy of library general rules, with corrections and annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of committee on University Playground and Athletic Field, transfer of the Medical College Laboratory in New York City to Cornell, donation of pensions by Carnegie Foundation, Chinese student scholarship, appeal for land lease for Sphinx Head Society

did.unittitle: Rolled student petition against the campus railway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 36 feet long

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes withdrawal of Sphinx Head Society petition, suggestion for reestablishment of Cornell Forest School, Sage College report, Seal and Serpent land lease petition, Sphinx Head and Quill and Dagger land lease petition, memoranda regarding grill work in Barnes Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lab receipts, documents on water pressure for fire uses, Medical College Council meeting, report of the Committee on Improvement of Salaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on proposed department abolishment in Sibley College, Cornellian Council appointments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Needs of the New York State Colleges at Cornell University", Sage College sprinkler report and diagram of piping, report on student drinking and misconduct in Ithaca, statement on death of Goldwin Smith, sale of Llenroc Estate, The Cornellian Council printed letter, Studie for Cornell on the location of buildings for the Agriculture College, and library fund memoranda

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter regarding Liberty Hyde Bailey as Commissioner of Agriculture, Bailey's report on the College of Agriculture, proposal for a busness farm or farm management farm, report on location of Risley College, photograph and plans of proposed college of Civil Engineering, report and blue print showing lines of proposed conduit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on proposed dormitory land, report of fraternity houses and dorms, formation of the Agricultural College Council, report on reorganization of State Agricultural College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of Goldwin Smith's Will, Cornellian Council budgets, library and Fiske money reports and resolutions, petition to appoint Lieut. Joseph Beacham Jr. as Commandant of the Military Department, inquiry on Sabbatic Leave for professors, printed report on Sibley College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to A.D. White on his birthday, report and synopsis of report of committee on commencement and reunion plans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information on faculty participation I nthe University Government, report of the Committee on Cost of Student Instruction and Tuition (with charts)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes retirement letter from Liberty Hyde Bailey and resolutions, medical college list of staff,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes inspections of fraternities and dormitories, letter from Madame Pansy Hadji Mischef asking for Bulgarian student scholarships, letters from the Cornell Women's Club of New York asking for more courses for women's vocations with endorsements and responses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposed statutes of Cornell, committee report on Cornell Women's Club petition recommending the appointment of a women as department chair, a pamphlet of rooms available in Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution of the Women's Club petition, Mr. Boldt's Luncheon seating chart, Report on vocational training for women, Cornell Calendar showing holidays and faculty meetings with number of hours per term committed to classes, printed budget packet for the College of Agriculture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the fraternities and dormitories, report of the proposed employment bureau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Mr. Boldt Luncheon seating chart, Memorandum of Faculty Participation in University Control, letter from New York Women's Club on Dean of Women at other universities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Kappa Alpha Theta asking to rent a house, letter from George Hamilton explaining the publication of an anti-war letter, resolution to appoint a Dean of Women, proposed revision of statutes, semi-centennial endowment fund, Ten Year Book resolutions and samples, Cornell Statutes of November 1916

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes War Department special orders, letter from Harriet Moody regarding Adviser of Women, by-laws of the Associate Alumni

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution on the occasion of Georges Mauxion's death in battle, report on undergraduate scholarships, report of the faculty in regard to the office of adviser of women

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters regarding changing the name of Sibley College, report of the Committee on University Publications, resolutions of the Cornell Chinese Students' Club, petitions for the appointment of Dean Smith as president, report of the Committee on the Reorganization of the Engineering Colleges, floor plan of dairy building, extract from Professor Orth's departmental report, report of Semi-Centennial Endowment Committee,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposed amendments to Cornell statutes, minutes of the committee on the Selection of a President and the Reorganization of the University, extract of Alma Webster's will,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter of retirement from George Lincoln Burr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition for consent to assignment of lease to Psi Upsilon and minutes, report of housing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlet of Statutes and Bylaws, report of the Council of the Delta Chi Alumni Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes rules and regulations from the Committee on Health, printed booklet of Cornell Statutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on death of Burt Green Wilder, housing inspection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes agreement between Society of New York Hospital and Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of "Build Yourself into Cornell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes request for funds for War records, prize competition publication,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes of the Board of Governors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on the faculty on compulsory military training, resolution to create the Provost's position,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed booklet on housing for men, amendments to the University Statutes,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Faculty Conference Committee on Military Training

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memorandum regarding proper corporate name of Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding Professor James Boyle's remarks about NYS Dairy farmers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Control of Traffic, Trespassing, and Vending on the Property of Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on Physical Recreation and Athletics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Arboretum Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes of Board on Student Health and Hygiene

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Committee on Law, resolutions on naval and defense leave of absence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes brochure "Cornell Women in the War", report of the special committee appointed to consider changes in the Cornell diploma, memorandum between Cornell and US Department of Agriculture relative to invesitgations on foods for Civilian and army use

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documentation (resume) for appointment of Vladimir Nabokov

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes first report of the Greater Cornell Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes discussion and report about Robinson Airlines (and Ithaca East Hill Airport), Exercises at the Laying of the Cornerstone of Statler Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes terms and recommendation of Deane Waldo Mallott as president

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes excerpt of report by acting president on Philip Morrison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes records establishing the Cornell University Archives, proposal to establish School of Industrial Labor Relations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Board on Traffic Control minutes, copy of The Cornell Graduate in Industry study

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes discussion of new men's dormitories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Deane Mallott's statement on deferred rushing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "From Now to 1960--A Preview", annual report of the president,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on increase in Women students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes statement to the Trustees by Jacob Gould Schurman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plans for research library (including floor plans)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter against destruction of Boardman Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes folder of election material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Student Government Constitution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Student Code

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of Industrial and Labor Relations faculty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding election of President Perkins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Unit Share System Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal to Air Force Office of Scientific Research for Continuation of Contract AF49(638)-1156

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes review of Synchrotron, discontent among students regarding education

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes announcements of Cornell Heights Residential Club fire, residential reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Presidential Committee on Military Training

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes personnel change of status

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Willard Straight Takeover resolutions and press releases, nullification of judicial procedures taken against students, statements by Perkins and Corson, statements on collection of firearms, State Act 7082, motion on racism task forces,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press releases on the president and personnel change of status

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes background reading for COSEP and Afro-American studies, personnel change in status

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Trustee Ad Hoc Committee on the Status of Minorities,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes packet of information on the Nguyen Cao Ky incident at Bailey Hall,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes President Rhodes' statement on the occupation of Day Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information on Zahorik et al v. Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Report of the Board Study Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes review of minority education at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes reports and information on South African Divestment

did.unittitle: Board of Trustees calendar and directory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a signed cabinet card of Ezra Cornell glued on the inside cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution on the formation of a Women's College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes comments and condolences following the death of Ezra Cornell

did.unittitle: Board retreat in Rye Brook, New York

p.emph: Files include correspondence, appropriations, conspectus, budget reports, appointments, resignations, applications, land sales/purchases, and tenure requests

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the librarian and list of donors, buildings report, accounts and expenses, trustee requests to Ezra Cornell to sell land scrip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence on sale of Jared Sparks library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence on sale of Jared Sparks library, private printing of "Some statements and Estimates of Immediate Wants Laid before the Trustees by the Scientific Professors and the Librarian, 1873"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding hours and uses of the library, anonymous letter regarding indecent bathing in the creek

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of committee on the regulations at Sage College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plan of organization of an Experiment Station

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on water safety, map of proposed lots, report of the committee on Veterinary Department

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal for Physics building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on conditions of Cascadilla Place, Report of the survey of Cornell Campus to George Schuyler by EA Fuertes (includes survey map), petition to decline resignation of William Russell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition to decline resignation of William Russell, petition from women to decline resignation of William Russell, EA Fuertes letter in regard to NY Times accusation, indenture between Cornell and Henry Sage for gymnasium lot, letter from AD White about purchase of casts, plan for Department of History and Political Science

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and sketch of Michigan University Library and White's sketch of proposed Cornell University Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drawing of campus with proposed walkways

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten chart of weather predictions for 1880-1882 by E. Fuertes from the signal station, sketch for Foundry and Blacksmith Hall, ratio of students to teachers in leading American colleges

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from faculty to provide baseball fields

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sketch of observatory by E.A. Fuertes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution establishing the Library Council, testimony of students involved in the destruction of the bridge.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes first annual report of the Archaeological Institute of America, E.A. Fuertes inventory of private property

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution to establish a museum council

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Charles Babcock about plans for a library,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolutions regarding the Jennie Fiske Hospital, copy of a pamphlet on Sage College for Women, copies of clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from students against dismissal of Professor McKoon, Psi Upsilon request for property, naming of White and Morrill Halls

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Burt Greene Wilder's application as curator of Vertebrate Zoology, proposal of lectures on forestry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from George W. Harris requesting to be made head of the library, report from AD White on the memorial windows in Sage Chapel, list of purchased plaster casts of Lincoln Cathedral

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes observatory estimates, letter about aid for students supporting themselves, resolution postponing the building of the observatory to a later date

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposal for a school of comparative palaeontology, petition from Bicycle Club asking permission to ride in the gymnasium, resolutions establishing the Gymnasium Council,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from the Board of Education of Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolutions on fellowships and scholarships,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes resolution and statutes on the establishment of a College of Pharmacy with letters from professors and University of Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from students to put a hand rail on the Willow Pond bridge, letter requesting renovations to the ladies room in Morrill, petition to reinstate Chi Psi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Charles Kendall Adams accepting the presidency

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of Committee on Salaries and Reorganization of Departments, Gluck's suggestions on increase of professor salaries and university reorganization,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes request for lot from Seabury Guild

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand drawn map of campus with professors' homes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Burt Green Wilder's requests for a manatee and other taxidermy animals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition for provisions for Roehrig's retirement,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes petition from the Tompkins County Agricultural Society for a building to be built for exhibitions, arrival of Edison's donation of the Dynamo-electric machine and installation, report on the proper organization of the Law Department

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes request to use the armory for military hops,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter on cadet rifles, report of the committee on Applied Science, list of seniors in civil engineering,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Burt Green Wilder regarding preservation of elephant brain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposed organization of the Department of Water Supply, Heat and Power, catalogue of books in library of W. Newcomb, letter from EA Fuertes on the poor condition of drinking water

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the physique of Cornell students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on disinfection of Sage following smallpox scare,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes library report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes map of proposed line of street Rail Road, report on the needs and conditions of Sibley College,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter requesting a room for childcare from wives of faculty,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sketched plan of Fall Creek dam and reservoir

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on Sibley College organization

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Committee of Senate on the chair of Rhetoric and English Philology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on Sage College, letter from Health Board of the university of the imminent danger of measles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of operations and power, dynamo plan from Sibley College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of courses of instruction in engineering

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes appointment of G.L. Burr as librarian of White Library with resolutions, statute establishing University Senate,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note from Thurston regarding poor water on campus, letter from George Warner regarding dangerous water in Cascadilla dorm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sketched plan of central campus, Lincoln Hall inscription written by AD White

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report on the Department of Political Economy and Finance, Memorandum concerning organization of the Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report from Sibley on arson of zoology building, diagrams and details of refitting McGraw departments and museum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes plan of University lighting

did.unittitle: Reports on Morrill-Nelson Land Grant, Department of Interior

Reports on Morrill-Nelson Land Grant, Department of Interior
Series IV. Council on the College of Veterinary Medicine
Series VI. Agricultural College Council and State College Council

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The State College Council was charged with governing the New York State Colleges, including the New York State College of Agriculture, New York State College of Home Economics, New York State Veterinary College, and New York State Agricultural Experiment Station. The Council consisted of several Cornell University trustees, the deans or directors of three of the colleges in question, two (or sometimes three) faculty representatives, and the University Comptroller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes university budget breakdown, Ezra Cornell donation resolution, invitation, and trustee election

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a report of reasons why the College of Agriculture needs reorganization and redirection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of minutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes university budget breakdown, Ezra Cornell donation resolution, invitation, and trustee election

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes map of distance of neighbors from Waite Barns

did.unittitle: State College Council Proceedings binder volume 1 and volume 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed meeting minutes bound in two small notebooks, with a typewritten name and subject index at the beginning of volume 1 and manuscript notes fastened in. Pages are numbered by hand. Topics covered in the minutes include the state colleges' finances, personnel changes (including appointments of county agricultural agents), and other administrative matters. Pages 316-318 at the end of volume 2 describe the discussion of the collection which became the Bailey Hortorium, the gift of Liberty Hyde Bailey.

did.unittitle: Bound Proceedings of State College Council
Bound Proceedings of State College Council
Bound Proceedings of State College Council
Series VII. Committee on Buildings and Grounds
